title: Fix virtual interface select from sub-interface instance (#7203)
body:
## Summary

Fixes #7203.

This patch fixes an internal error when selecting a sub-interface instance
through a virtual interface handle, e.g. `vif.sub_if` or chained access
like `vif.sub_if.member`.

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
interface sub_if (output reg Tx);
endinterface

interface top_if (output reg Tx);
  sub_if tx (Tx);
endinterface

package pkg;
  typedef virtual top_if vif_t;
  class driver_c;
    vif_t vif;
    function void f();
      vif.tx.Tx = 1'b1;
    endfunction
  endclass
endpackage

module t;
endmodule
```

On master:

```
%Error: Internal Error: V3Width.cpp: MemberSel of non-variable
```

## Changes

- `V3Width.cpp`: Add `AstCell` handler in `IfaceRefDType` MemberSel processing
  to resolve sub-interface cells via their `__Viftop` companion variable.
- `V3Width.cpp`: Use `ifaceViaCellp()` instead of `ifacep()` in
  `memberSelIface()` to support chained sub-interface access.
- `V3Width.cpp`: Include `AstCell` in `memberSelIface()` spellcheck
  candidates.
- `V3EmitCFunc.h`: Emit cell name (not `__Viftop` var name) for
  `IFACEREF` MemberSel to match the C++ class member.

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_interface_virtual_sub_iface.{v,py}` -- sub-interface
  select, chained member access, read/write through sub-interface.

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
